seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has had recurrent headaches since adolescence
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches were previously associated with menstrual periods
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches are now triggered both around menses and by stress
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Recently the patient's headaches have become more frequent
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Recently the patient's headaches have become more severe
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's primary care provider referred her for evaluation of headaches
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches are mostly on the right side of the head
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches feel like a pulsing sensation behind the right eye
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has been trying to exercise more in order to lose weight
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches worsen shortly after starting to jog
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has had ringing in the ears for a few months
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has intermittent blurred vision affecting the side of her visual field
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient denies numbness or tingling in hands, feet, or face
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient runs for exercise and is getting back into running
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient currently uses Asics running shoes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient had a brain MRI a few years ago
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The primary care physician recently ordered a new brain MRI but it has not yet been scheduled
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has not yet been called to schedule the new brain MRI
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician reviewed the patient's vital signs and they were normal, including blood pressure
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: On cardiac exam the patient has normal sinus rhythm without murmurs, rubs, or gallops
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Funduscopic exam shows papilledema
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Neurological exam shows cranial nerves I through XII intact with normal corneal reflex
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Musculoskeletal exam shows normal gait
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician is concerned the patient has idiopathic intracranial hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician states that idiopathic intracranial hypertension fits the patient's symptoms of headaches worsened with activity, tinnitus, and visual changes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician is concerned that ongoing increased intracranial pressure could cause permanent vision changes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to obtain a brain MRI to evaluate for other conditions contributing to the patient's symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to perform a lumbar puncture to measure intracranial pressure
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician explains that lumbar puncture may provide symptomatic relief by reducing intracranial pressure
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient asks whether confirmation of idiopathic intracranial hypertension would mean she needs surgery
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician states that idiopathic intracranial hypertension does not necessarily require surgery
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician states there are medications that can decrease intracranial pressure in idiopathic intracranial hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician explains that lowering intracranial pressure with medication can reduce the patient's headaches, including with exercise
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician counsels that exercise and weight loss are important parts of treatment for idiopathic intracranial hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to obtain insurance preapproval for the MRI
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician's office staff will contact the patient to schedule the MRI after preapproval
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient
